chunk_summary: This section focuses on capturing diet history, conducting physical exams, and documenting actionable findings to address nutrition concerns in children, emphasizing structured interventions and adherence strategies in clinical settings.
learning_objectives: Understand how to conduct a detailed diet history, including 24-hour recall and feeding dynamics.
Identify reversible causes of nutrition-related issues through targeted physical examination.
Develop structured intervention plans and documentation for nutrition counseling in pediatric cases.
Recognize red flags requiring further investigation or referral in growth and feeding concerns.
key_facts: A 24-hour diet recall should include portion sizes, meal timing, cooking methods, and liquid calorie intake.
Screen for medical contributors like constipation, reflux, chronic infections, malabsorption, and psychosocial stressors such as food insecurity and caregiver mental health issues.
Physical exams should assess hydration, edema, oral health, micronutrient deficiencies, and neurodevelopmental impairments affecting feeding skills.
Structured interventions should last 2–4 weeks for behavior-driven concerns before escalating to investigations or referrals.
Use a “3-item prescription” approach: one behavior target, one food substitution, and one follow-up date to improve adherence in busy outpatient settings.
reference_quote: Recognition and management of faltering growth in children should include targeted interventions and structured follow-ups. (NICE NG75)
document_key_topics: Screening for medical and psychosocial contributors
